hotel.name: Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows
hotel.description: The most affordable beachfront option at Hat Thung Wua Laen — simple thatched bungalows in a shaded garden right on the sand. A classic Thai beach bungalow setup that's getting rarer on the country's more developed coasts. Fans or basic AC, cold or hot water depending on the room tier. The open-air restaurant serves fresh seafood for dinner. No frills, no pool — just a beach bungalow with your feet in the sand.
highlights: Authentic beach bungalow atmosphere directly on Hat Thung Wua Laen
Most affordable beachfront option on the beach

similarHotels.name: Faasai Resort & Spa
similarHotels.description: A consistently well-reviewed budget guesthouse in Chumphon town near the main market and train station. Simple but clean fan and air-conditioned rooms in a converted shophouse with a friendly Thai family running the operation. Breakfast available on-site for 100-150 THB. Ideal for travelers catching early morning ferries to Koh Tao — the Lomprayah office is a short walk.
highlights: Walking distance to train station and Lomprayah office
Friendly owners who help arrange ferry tickets and motorbike rentals
Clean rooms with reliable hot water and WiFi

similarHotels.name: Retro Box Hotel Chumphon
similarHotels.description: A modern budget hotel with a boutique twist — container-style design, clean contemporary rooms with AC, an outdoor swimming pool, and free parking. Located a 9-minute walk from Chumphon train station, making it practical for rail arrivals. One of the best-reviewed budget properties in town, standing out for its swimming pool at a price point where pools are unusual.
highlights: Outdoor swimming pool — rare at this price in Chumphon
Modern design with comfortable beds and reliable AC

similarHotels.name: HOP INN Chumphon
similarHotels.description: Thailand's reliable budget chain hotel brand with a branch in Chumphon town. Simple, clean, and standardized — what you see online is exactly what you get. Air-conditioned rooms, comfortable beds, free WiFi, and a predictable standard that suits travelers who don't want surprises. A 11-minute walk from the train station. No pool but good value for the consistency.
highlights: Reliable Thai hotel chain — consistent quality nationwide
Affordable AC rooms in central Chumphon
11-minute walk to Chumphon train station and Lomprayah office

aiContent.hookIntro: Booking Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows without knowing the lack of a pool can leave you missing a swim option on hot days.
aiContent.intro: Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows offers simple thatched bungalows in a shaded garden right on Hat Thung Wua Laen sand, with fans or basic AC and cold or hot water depending on the room. It's ideal for backpackers and budget travelers seeking an authentic Thai beach experience without modern frills.
whyBook: Authentic thatched bungalows directly on the beach
Most affordable beachfront stay at 600-1,100 THB/night
Shaded garden setting for relaxed nature vibes
Fresh seafood at the open-air beachfront restaurant
whyNotBook: No pool for cooling off during midday heat
Basic rooms lack modern amenities like reliable WiFi
Fan-only options in lower tiers feel stuffy at night
Limited room tiers mean hot water not guaranteed
No on-site activities beyond beach lounging
aiContent.skipIf: Skip if you need air-conditioned comfort and pool access in a more developed resort area.
aiContent.detailedReview: Checking in at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows feels like stepping into a classic Thai beach hideaway, with a casual front desk in the shaded garden welcoming backpackers with minimal fuss. Rooms are simple thatched bungalows scattered among tropical trees, right on the Hat Thung Wua Laen sand—some with fans and cold showers for the budget tier, others adding basic AC and hot water for a slight upgrade. The vibe is rustic and unpretentious, evoking rarer beach setups as Thailand's coasts develop elsewhere. Standout is the direct beach access, where you can wake up and have your feet in the sand within seconds. The open-air restaurant serves fresh seafood dinners, like grilled fish caught nearby, in a laid-back setting overlooking the waves—portions are generous but expect simple preparations without gourmet flair. Service is friendly and low-key, typical of family-run spots, with staff helping with basic needs like towel changes but not hovering. Guests here are mostly backpackers, budget travelers, and nature lovers in their 20s-40s, drawn to the quiet escape from Chumphon's busier town spots. Evenings bring a communal feel around the restaurant, though days can feel idle without organized activities. Overall, it's a no-frills stay that delivers on affordability and authenticity, but don't expect polished comforts—power outages or basic plumbing quirks can occur in this remote beachfront setup.
aiContent.locationReality: Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows sits directly on Hat Thung Wua Laen beach, a quieter stretch in Chumphon province about 40km south of Chumphon town. Within a 5-minute walk, you'll find the sandy shoreline and a few local seafood shacks, but the nearest convenience store or market requires a 10-15 minute motorbike ride. For Chumphon town's train station or pier, expect a 45-60 minute drive costing 500-800 THB by taxi or songthaew.
localSignals: Directly on Hat Thung Wua Laen beach—0 minutes to sand
Nearest landmark: Thung Wua Laen Beach viewpoint, 5-minute walk
Chumphon Airport transfer: about 1-hour drive, 1,000-1,500 THB
No 7-Eleven within 5-min walk; nearest in Sai Ri Sawi, 10-min ride
aiContent.travellerNote: Guests often praise the authentic beach bungalow atmosphere and affordable direct sand access but note the lack of a pool and basic room facilities as common drawbacks.
bookingTips: Request AC and hot water rooms for comfort over fan tiers
Agoda often cheaper than Booking.com for this property in Thailand
Prices spike December-February high season due to beach holidays
Verify seafood restaurant hours as it closes early off-season
Check for any construction notices on bungalows via direct email

similarHotels.name: Faasai Resort & Spa
similarHotels.howDifferent: Inland Chumphon town near market and train, lacks beachfront but adds spa elements.

similarHotels.name: Retro Box Hotel Chumphon
similarHotels.howDifferent: Modern container design in town with pool, but no beach access or bungalow vibe.

similarHotels.name: HOP INN Chumphon
similarHotels.howDifferent: Standardized chain in town center, clean and reliable but far from beaches.

faq.q: Does Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows have a pool?
faq.a: No, there is no pool—guests rely on the beach for swimming and cooling off.

faq.q: What types of rooms are available at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows?
faq.a: Simple thatched bungalows with fans and cold water in basic tiers, or basic AC and hot water in upgraded ones.

faq.q: Is breakfast included at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows?
faq.a: Breakfast is not mentioned as included; the open-air restaurant focuses on dinner seafood.

faq.q: How far is Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows from Chumphon town?
faq.a: It's about 40km south on Hat Thung Wua Laen, requiring a 45-60 minute drive.

faq.q: Is WiFi available at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows?
faq.a: WiFi status varies and is not guaranteed in this basic beachfront setup.

faq.q: What food options are there at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows?
faq.a: The beachfront restaurant serves fresh seafood for dinner in an open-air setting.

aiContent.urgencyLine: Beachfront bungalows at Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows book out 1-2 months ahead for December-February high season—check today.
aiContent.methodology: We rated Long Beach Garden Hotel & Bungalows based on location authenticity, affordability, and guest suitability for budget beach stays. We weighted beach access highest because it defines the property's core appeal in Chumphon's coastal scene. Prices and availability change—always verify before booking. Last updated: 2026-04.
